mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various AI frameworks, providing a standardized approach to agent communication and management. Use Kagenti when you need a robust platform for AI agent deployment that addresses security and operational challenges.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the Kagenti deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to the local bin directory.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to access the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Install Kagenti on the cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys Kagenti to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Adds the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Installs Kagenti in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Post-installation configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies the autoscaling configuration.
> Applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> This command uninstalls the Kagenti Helm release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step will upgrade Kagenti to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Backs up the current state of the Kagenti deployment.
> Upgrades the Kagenti deployment to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if your kubeconfig is not set up correctly or if the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1' to update your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install chart: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Helm repository is not added correctly or if the version is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the repository is added with 'helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti' and check the version.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the application inside the pod is failing to start.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kagenti' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This occurs when the cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI agent protocols and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kagenti' showing all pods in 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
